aiTitle: Add public methods to ContinuousRotate component

content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>我覺得ContinuousRotate.ts應該要有提供函式可暫停旋轉或是變慢加快等公開函式</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-09</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
完成。新檔 `ContinuousRotate.ts` 型別檢查與 IDE 診斷都乾淨（唯一報錯是 tsconfig 既有的 `node10` deprecation，與本次無關）。

## 做了什麼

`assets/Script/Common/ContinuousRotate.ts` — 一個通用的持續旋轉元件：
- 掛到任何節點 → 該節點繞 Z 軸永遠自轉
- 可設「一圈秒數」「順時針/逆時針」
- 用 component 的 `enabled` 開關控制（停就取消勾選），不另設多餘 API
- 跟 `FeatureWheelManager` **零耦合**，PlaySpin 暫停輪盤時它照轉 ✅（符合你選的「完全獨立、永遠轉」）

## 你還需要在編輯器做（Cocos 端，程式碼動不了）

1. 選中魔法字環節點 → Add Component → `ContinuousRotate`
2. 設「一圈秒數」（建議比輪盤 Idle 的 60 快/慢一點做出層次感）與方向

## 兩個要提醒你的點

**① `FeatureWheelManager` 的 `m_magicWordRingNode`（line 73-74）現在是無用 property。**
選了方案 A 後，旋轉由獨立元件處理，manager 不再需要這個引用。它是這次任務前就預留、目前整支檔案只宣告沒使用。建議移除以免留一個 dangling property——但**移除會動到 prefab 序列化**（要連帶在 prefab 上重新確認），所以我沒擅自動。要我移除嗎？

**② 節點層級的疊加風險（請你在編輯器確認）：**
若魔法字環節點在 prefab 裡是 `m_wheelNode`（輪盤旋轉節點）的**子節點**，它會繼承輪盤旋轉，再自轉就會**疊加**（兩者角速度相加），視覺會怪。要它是輪盤的**同層/獨立節點**，單獨自轉才正確。

---
合理性審查：元件單一職責、命名與在地風格一致、防角度漂移與既有輪盤同款。沒有 over-design（YAGNI：未加轉軸選項/目標節點引用，需要再加）。

要我幫你移除 `m_magicWordRingNode` 那個無用 property 嗎？（會一併說明 prefab 端要注意什麼）

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The user request I'm observing is in Chinese (Traditional):
"我覺得ContinuousRotate.ts應該要有提供函式可暫停旋轉或是變慢加快等公開函式"

Translation: "I think ContinuousRotate.ts should provide functions to pause rotation or slow down/speed up and other public functions"

This is a user request/suggestion about a feature that should be added to ContinuousRotate.ts. The user wants public functions to:
1. Pause rotation
2. Slow down/speed up rotation
3. Other control functions

This represents a decision or feature request that the user is making. Since I'm observing what was learned/built/fixed/deployed, I should record this as a decision about what functionality ContinuousRotate.ts should provide.

Type: This is a "decision" - the user has decided what functionality should be added to ContinuousRotate.ts

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>ContinuousRotate.ts needs public control functions</title>
  <subtitle>User identified need for pause, speed control, and other public functions in ContinuousRotate.ts</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ContinuousRotate.ts should expose public function to pause rotation</fact>
    <fact>ContinuousRotate.ts should expose public functions to slow down or speed up rotation</fact>
    <fact>Additional public control functions needed for rotation manipulation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The user identified that ContinuousRotate.ts currently lacks essential public API functions for controlling rotation behavior. The component needs exposed methods to pause rotation, adjust rotation speed (both faster and slower), and potentially other control functions. This decision reflects a requirement for programmatic control over continuous rotation behavior beyond just starting the rotation.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

aiTitle: Add public methods to ContinuousRotate component

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Write</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-09T08:37:36.430Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_872_eyestrike2_client</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"g:\\\\Cocos_Project\\\\uk_872_eyestrike2_client\\\\assets\\\\Script\\\\Common\\\\ContinuousRotate.ts\",\"content\":\"import { _decorator, Component } from 'cc';\\n\\nconst { ccclass, property } = _decorator;\\n\\n/**\\n * @ch 持續旋轉元件（通用 · self-contained）\\n * 單一職責：掛上後讓所屬節點繞 Z 軸永遠自轉，與任何業務邏輯零耦合。\\n * - 適用：魔法字環、裝飾光環等需要「不斷旋轉」的節點\\n * - 控制：可在 Inspector 設初值；執行期用公開 API（Pause/Resume/SetSecondsPerRev/SetClockwise）動態調整\\n * - 不負責：落點/停輪/tween 演出（那是 RouletteRotate 的職責）\\n */\\n@ccclass('ContinuousRotate')\\nexport class ContinuousRotate extends Component {\\n    //#region 屬性\\n    /**@ch 一圈秒數（越大越慢；≤0 視為不轉）*/\\n    @property({ displayName: '一圈秒數' })\\n    private m_secondsPerRev: number = 60;\\n\\n    /**@ch 轉動方向：勾選=順時針，取消=逆時針（順時針 = z 遞減，與專案其他輪盤一致）*/\\n    @property({ displayName: '順時針轉動' })\\n    private m_clockwise: boolean = true;\\n    //#endregion 屬性\\n\\n    //#region 變數\\n    /**@ch 是否暫停（暫停時保留當前角度，Resume 從原處續轉）*/\\n    private m_paused: boolean = false;\\n    //#endregion 變數\\n\\n    //#region 生命週期\\n    update(dt: number) {\\n        if (this.m_paused || this.m_secondsPerRev <= 0) {\\n            return;\\n        }\\n        const dir = this.m_clockwise ? -1 : 1;\\n        const deltaAngle = dir * (360 / this.m_secondsPerRev) * dt;\\n        // 正規化防連續累積漂移（與 FeatureWheel/RouletteRotate 收尾一致）\\n        const next = this.NormalizeAngle(this.node.eulerAngles.z + deltaAngle);\\n        this.node.setRotationFromEuler(0, 0, next);\\n    }\\n    //#endregion 生命週期\\n\\n    //#region 公開 API\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 暫停旋轉（保留當前角度，不重置）\\n     */\\n    public Pause(): void {\\n        this.m_paused = true;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 恢復旋轉（從當前角度續轉）\\n     */\\n    public Resume(): void {\\n        this.m_paused = false;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 是否正在旋轉（未暫停且速度有效）\\n     */\\n    public IsRotating(): boolean {\\n        return !this.m_paused && this.m_secondsPerRev > 0;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 設定轉速（一圈秒數；越大越慢，≤0 視為不轉）\\n     * 用途：動態變快/變慢，例：SetSecondsPerRev(30) 比預設 60 快一倍\\n     * @param seconds 一圈所需秒數\\n     */\\n    public SetSecondsPerRev(seconds: number): void {\\n        this.m_secondsPerRev = seconds;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 設定轉動方向\\n     * @param clockwise true=順時針，false=逆時針\\n     */\\n    public SetClockwise(clockwise: boolean): void {\\n        this.m_clockwise = clockwise;\\n    }\\n    //#endregion 公開 API\\n\\n    //#region 方法\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 將角度正規化到 [0, 360)\\n     */\\n    private NormalizeAngle(angle: number): number {\\n        return ((angle % 360) + 360) % 360;\\n    }\\n    //#endregion 方法\\n}\\n\"}"</parameters>

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to generate a progress summary for what happened in the observed session. Let me analyze what was done:

1. **Request**: User (in Chinese) said that ContinuousRotate.ts should provide public functions to pause rotation or change speed (slow down/speed up)

2. **What Claude did in the primary session**: 
   - Created a new file `assets/Script/Common/ContinuousRotate.ts`
   - This is a generic continuous rotation component for Cocos Creator
   - It rotates any node around Z-axis continuously
   - Can set "seconds per revolution" and "clockwise/counterclockwise"
   - Uses component's `enabled` property for control (instead of adding extra APIs)
   - Zero coupling with `FeatureWheelManager`
   - When PlaySpin pauses the wheel, this magic word ring keeps rotating (Option A - completely independent, always rotating)

3. **What needs to be done in editor**:
   - Select magic word ring node → Add Component → `ContinuousRotate`
   - Set seconds per revolution and direction

4. **Two important points raised**:
   - `m_magicWordRingNode` in `FeatureWheelManager` (lines 73-74) is now unused property - should it be removed?
   - Risk of rotation stacking if magic word ring node is a child of the wheel node - needs to be sibling/independent

5. **Learned**:
   - Component follows single responsibility principle
   - Naming consistent with local style
   - Has angle drift prevention like existing wheel
   - No over-design (YAGNI principle applied)

Interestingly, the user's initial request was about adding pause/speed control functions, but Claude implemented it differently - using the `enabled` property for pause control rather than adding explicit pause functions. This is a design decision.
Now I should write the summary of what was accomplished. The core achievement is delivering a reusable rotation component that decouples the magic word ring from the wheel manager entirely. Rather than adding explicit pause/speed methods, leveraging the component's built-in `enabled` flag provides a cleaner interface. Two cleanup considerations emerged: removing the now-redundant `m_magicWordRingNode` property and ensuring the ring node isn't nested under the wheel to avoid compounded rotations.

content.text:
```xml
<summary>
  <request>Add public control functions to ContinuousRotate.ts for pausing and speed adjustment</request>
  <investigated>Explored how to implement rotation control for the magic word ring component in a Cocos Creator game, evaluating whether to add explicit pause/speed control APIs versus using built-in component properties</investigated>
  <learned>ContinuousRotate.ts was implemented with component's built-in `enabled` property for pause control instead of custom pause functions. The component rotates nodes around Z-axis continuously with configurable seconds-per-revolution and direction. It maintains zero coupling with FeatureWheelManager, allowing independent rotation even when the main wheel is paused. The implementation includes angle drift prevention similar to existing wheel logic and follows single responsibility principle without over-engineering.</learned>
  <completed>Created new file `assets/Script/Common/ContinuousRotate.ts` as a generic continuous rotation component. Type checking and IDE diagnostics are clean. The component can be attached to any node for continuous Z-axis rotation with configurable speed and direction. Control mechanism uses component's `enabled` toggle rather than custom APIs.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting decision on whether to remove unused `m_magicWordRingNode` property from FeatureWheelManager (lines 73-74), which would require prefab serialization changes. User needs to add ContinuousRotate component to magic word ring node in Cocos editor and verify node hierarchy to avoid rotation stacking if ring is child of wheel node.</next_steps>
  <notes>Design decision made to use component's built-in `enabled` property for pause control rather than implementing custom pause/resume functions, keeping the API minimal and following framework conventions. Identified potential node hierarchy issue where magic word ring must be sibling to wheel node rather than child to avoid rotation inheritance/stacking.</notes>
</summary>
```

方法\",\"     /**\",\"      * @ch 將角度正規化到 [0, 360)\"]}],\"originalFile\":\"import { _decorator, Component } from 'cc';\\n\\nconst { ccclass, property } = _decorator;\\n\\n/**\\n * @ch 持續旋轉元件（通用 · self-contained）\\n * 單一職責：掛上後讓所屬節點繞 Z 軸永遠自轉，與任何業務邏輯零耦合。\\n * - 適用：魔法字環、裝飾光環等需要「不斷旋轉」的節點\\n * - 控制：以 component 的 enabled 開關（enabled=false 即停，built-in，不另設 API）\\n * - 不負責：落點/停輪/tween 演出（那是 aiTitle: Add public methods to ContinuousRotate component

content.thinking:
The user has updated ContinuousRotate.ts to add the public control functions they requested. Let me analyze what changed:

**Changes made:**
1. Added a new private variable `m_paused: boolean = false` to track pause state
2. Updated the `update` method to check for `this.m_paused` in addition to `this.m_secondsPerRev <= 0`
3. Added public API functions:
   - `Pause()`: sets `m_paused` to true
   - `Resume()`: sets `m_paused` to false
   - `IsRotating()`: returns whether the component is currently rotating (not paused and speed is valid)
   - `SetSecondsPerRev(seconds: number)`: allows dynamic speed adjustment
   - `SetClockwise(clockwise: boolean)`: allows dynamic direction change
4. Updated the class documentation comment from "控制：以 component 的 enabled 開關（enabled=false 即停，built-in，不另設 API）" to "控制：可在 Inspector 設初值；執行期用公開 API（Pause/Resume/SetSecondsPerRev/SetClockwise）動態調整"

This is a **change** type observation (or possibly **feature** since it adds new capabilities). It's adding the requested public control functions for pausing and speed control.

Let me create an observation for this.

content.text:

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added public control API to ContinuousRotate component</title>
  <subtitle>Implemented pause, resume, speed adjustment, and direction control functions for dynamic rotation manipulation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added Pause() and Resume() public methods to control rotation state without destroying the component</fact>
    <fact>Added SetSecondsPerRev(seconds: number) method to dynamically adjust rotation speed at runtime</fact>
    <fact>Added SetClockwise(clockwise: boolean) method to dynamically change rotation direction</fact>
    <fact>Added IsRotating() query method to check if component is actively rotating</fact>
    <fact>Internal m_paused flag preserves current angle when paused and resumes from same position</fact>
    <fact>Update method now checks both m_paused state and m_secondsPerRev validity before rotating</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>ContinuousRotate.ts was enhanced from a simple enabled/disabled component to a fully controllable rotation system with runtime API. The component now exposes five public methods: Pause() to stop rotation while preserving angle, Resume() to continue from current position, IsRotating() to query rotation state, SetSecondsPerRev() to dynamically adjust speed (e.g., SetSecondsPerRev(30) makes it twice as fast as default 60), and SetClockwise() to reverse direction. This replaces the initial design which relied solely on the component's built-in enabled property for control. The pause mechanism uses an internal m_paused flag that halts rotation in the update loop while maintaining the current angle, allowing seamless resume from the same position.</narrative>
  <concepts>
